topic: Volkswagen emissions scandal (Dieselgate) — public trust in VW

events.description: Researchers from West Virginia University and the ICCT publish a study revealing that VW diesel vehicles emit nitrogen oxides at levels significantly higher than lab results during real-world driving.
The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) issues a Notice of Violation to Volkswagen, publicly accusing the company of using 'defeat device' software to bypass emissions standards.
Volkswagen officially admits that the 'defeat device' software is installed in approximately 11 million vehicles worldwide, confirming the scale of the deception.
Volkswagen CEO Martin Winterkorn resigns from his position, taking responsibility for the scandal while maintaining he was unaware of the specific wrongdoing.
Volkswagen reaches a massive settlement with U.S. regulators and vehicle owners, agreeing to pay billions to resolve claims related to the emissions cheating.
Total financial impact of the scandal reaches a cumulative peak, reflecting the long-term cost of fines, legal settlements, and vehicle buybacks.

stakeholders.name: Martin Winterkorn
stakeholders.position: He issued a formal apology for breaking the trust of the public and customers, stating he was deeply sorry for the deception while initially claiming he was unaware of the wrongdoing.

stakeholders.name: Matthias Müller
stakeholders.position: He pledged to make Volkswagen a more honest, open, and modern company, emphasizing a cultural shift to regain public trust after the scandal.

stakeholders.name: Gina McCarthy
stakeholders.role: Administrator of the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA)
stakeholders.position: She condemned Volkswagen's actions as a direct threat to public health and a blatant violation of the Clean Air Act, stating the agency would hold the company accountable.

outcome_description: While the scandal caused a severe initial collapse in brand reputation and public trust, Volkswagen successfully navigated the crisis through aggressive financial settlements and a strategic pivot toward electric vehicles, allowing the company to recover its market position and consumer standing over time.

key_figures.emissions_limit_exceedance: up to 40 times the legal limit
